name: Las Zetas de la Pedriza Loop
name_original: Collado de los Pastores y Nava Grande – circular desde Manzanares el Real por camino Las Zetas de la Pedriza
description: Tackle the difficult Las Zetas de la Pedriza Loop, a 30.1-mile mountain biking route with 4404 feet of elevation gain in La Pedriza.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Manzanares El Real offers diverse terrain within a UNESCO-declared biosphere reserve, the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park. The region is characterized by dramatically eroded granitic outcrops of La Pedriza, rocky cliffs, and extensive pine forests of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Riders can explore trails ranging from challenging singletrack with significant elevation changes to more accessible paths around the Santillana Reservoir. This area provides a rich blend of natural beauty and adventure opportunities for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Manzanares El Real The most popular mountain bike trail is Las Zetas de la Pedriza y Manzanares el Real — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 36.7 miles (59.1 km) trail that takes 6 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through granite rock formations and varied landscapes including gorges and pine forests. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Las Zetas de la Pedriza Loop, a difficult 30.1 miles (48.5 km) path. This route features challenging terrain and significant elevation gain within La Pedriza. Local mountain bikers also love the Santillana Reservoir – Santillana Reservoir Bridge loop from El Boalo-Cerceda-Mataelpino, an easy 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through a rich ecosystem around the reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Mountain biking around Manzanares El Real is defined by granitic outcrops, rocky cliffs, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy routes around the reservoir to advanced mountain biking routes with substantial elevation gain. The routes in Manzanares El Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 190 reviews. More than 2600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Manzanares El Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Manzanares El Real?
A: Manzanares El Real offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from easy routes around the Santillana Reservoir to very difficult paths with significant elevation changes within La Pedriza and the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. You'll encounter challenging singletrack, granite rock formations, and trails through pine forests and gorges.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families in Manzanares El Real?
A: Yes, there are family-friendly options. For an easier ride, consider routes around the Santillana Reservoir. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23944516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir – Santillana Reservoir Bridge loop from El Boalo-Cerceda-Mataelpino&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 13.4-mile (21.6 km) trail offering pleasant views and a rich ecosystem.

Q: What are the most challenging mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1348735288/las-zetas-de-la-pedriza-y-manzanares-el-real-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Zetas de la Pedriza y Manzanares el Real — Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt; (36.7 miles / 59.1 km) and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961656320/las-zetas-de-la-pedriza-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Zetas de la Pedriza Loop&lt;/a&gt; (30.1 miles / 48.5 km) are highly recommended. These difficult routes feature challenging terrain, granite rock formations, and substantial elevation gains within La Pedriza.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Manzanares El Real?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the dramatic granitic outcrops of La Pedriza, ride through the pine forests of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3692534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalara Glacial Cirque&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Manzanares El Real are circular. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961656320/las-zetas-de-la-pedriza-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Zetas de la Pedriza Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging circular experience. An easier option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23944516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir – Santillana Reservoir Bridge loop from El Boalo-Cerceda-Mataelpino&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Manzanares El Real?
A: The mountain bike trails in Manzanares El Real are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 190 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from challenging granite formations to scenic reservoir paths, and the unparalleled natural surroundings.

Q: Is it possible to reach Manzanares El Real by public transport for mountain biking?
A: Manzanares El Real is accessible by public transport from Madrid, typically by bus. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycle transport before planning your trip.

Q: Are there specific permits required for mountain biking in certain areas like La Pedriza?
A: Manzanares El Real is part of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park and borders the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. While many trails are freely accessible, some sensitive areas within these parks, especially for activities like climbing, may have specific regulations or require permits. It's always best to check with the park authorities for the latest information on mountain biking restrictions or permit requirements for specific routes, particularly within La Pedriza.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Manzanares El Real?
A: The region offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters can bring snow to higher parts of the Sierra de Guadarrama, making some trails more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails in Manzanares El Real?
A: Yes, the town of Manzanares El Real itself has various cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel after your ride. Many are conveniently located near trailheads or within easy reach of the town center.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Manzanares El Real?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Manzanares El Real, particularly near popular trail access points and the town center. Specific parking areas may be designated for visitors to La Pedriza or the Santillana Reservoir, so look for signs upon arrival.

Q: Are there any other notable attractions to visit in Manzanares El Real besides mountain biking?
A: Absolutely. You can visit the impressive 15th-century Mendoza Castle, considered one of the best-preserved fortresses in the Madrid region. The Visitor Centre for La Pedriza also offers information and exhibitions on the Sierra de Guadarrama. For more general information about the town, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visitacity.com/en/manzanares-el-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit a City - Manzanares El Real&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
The Collado de los Pastores is a beautiful landmark in the Sierra de Guadarrama, located in the area of La Pedriza, near Manzanares el Real, in the Community of Madrid. This hill offers stunning panoramic views of the region, including the Bola del Mundo, the Cuerda Larga and the granite Pedriza.
One of the popular routes to reach the Collado de los Pastores begins at the Canto Cochino car park. From there, you ascend the Cuerda de los Porrones, passing by the Mirador de la Pedriza, until you reach the hill.
Be careful with the descent, it can be dangerous because the bike picks up a lot of speed
Spectacular viewpoint overlooking the entire Pedriza and half of Madrid. The final climb is tough, but worth it
After the zetas, a good place to regain strength
a demanding but very rewarding climb due to the views of the valley that can be seen, and of Pedriza itself.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Las Zetas de la Pedriza Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop is typically accessed from Manzanares el Real, which offers various parking options. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, as parking can fill up quickly due to the area's popularity.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route, and how difficult is it?
faqs.answer: The route is largely classified as a hard mountain bike ride, demanding a very good level of fitness. It primarily traverses forest tracks, with some initial asphalted sections. A significant feature is a challenging 9-kilometer climb with numerous 'zetas' (switchbacks). Beyond this, expect undulating 'sube-baja' (up and down) sections and a long, enjoyable descent. Riders should be prepared for technical sections (S0-S1 to S3-S6) where dismounting and pushing may be necessary. It's suitable for mountain bikes, and some sections can accommodate gravel bikes.

faqs.question: What notable features and viewpoints can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers magnificent panoramic views of the unique Pedriza cirque with its striking granite formations. Key highlights include the demanding 9-kilometer 'Zetas' climb, the expansive vistas from Collado de los Pastores, and the Viewpoint of the Zetas of La Pedriza. You'll also pass through pine forests and high mountain pastures, with views dominated by La Maliciosa and the peaks of Cuerda Larga. An optional ascent to La Nava Grande Summit provides an additional challenge and further elevation gain.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is generally not suitable for beginners or young families. It is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride requiring a very good level of fitness and advanced riding skills due to its length, significant elevation gain, and technical sections. While some parts might be easier, the overall loop presents a considerable challenge.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Optimal enjoyment of the scenic beauty and more stable weather conditions are typically found during spring and autumn. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring snow and ice, especially at higher elevations, making conditions more challenging and requiring specific gear. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Is the Las Zetas de la Pedriza Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please also ensure you carry bags to pick up after your dog.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Las Zetas de la Pedriza Loop itself. However, as it's part of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, visitors are expected to adhere to the park's regulations.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when mountain biking in Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
faqs.answer: When mountain biking in Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, it's crucial to stay on marked trails to protect the natural environment. Respect wildlife, do not litter, and be mindful of other users, as the trails are popular with hikers, E-bikers, gravel bikers, and trail runners. Dogs must be kept on a leash. Open fires are strictly prohibited, and camping is regulated. Always check the official park website for the most current regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers a significant portion of the official trail named Las Zetas de la Pedriza. It also passes through sections of the Senda circular Sierra de los Porrones - Arroyo Manzanares, Río Manzanares, and Senda de las Cabras, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and length of the route, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing layers for varying weather conditions. A helmet is essential, and protective gear is highly recommended. A GPS device or a reliable map (like the komoot app) is also crucial for navigation.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, Manzanares el Real, the town from which the loop is typically accessed, offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel before or after your ride.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of the Zetas of La Pedriza
